message.content:
Search the web and find comprehensive information about the "Harness Protocol" - this appears to be a development methodology or protocol for AI-assisted development with Claude Code. 

Look for:
1. What is the Harness Protocol?
2. How does it work with Claude Code hooks?
3. Best practices for setting it up
4. Any documentation or examples

Search terms to try:
- "Harness Protocol Claude Code"
- "Harness Protocol AI development"
- "Claude Code harness hooks"

Provide a comprehensive summary of what you find.

Links: [{"title":"Streamline feature management with Harness MCP and Claude Code","url":"https://www.harness.io/blog/ai-powered-feature-management-with-harness-mcp-server-and-claude-code"},{"title":"GitHub - elct9620/ccharness: A lightweight harness for Claude Code","url":"https://github.com/elct9620/ccharness"},{"title":"The Claude Code SDK and the Birth of HaaS (Harness as a Service) | vtrivedy","url":"https://www.vtrivedy.com/posts/claude-code-sdk-haas-harness-as-a-service/"},{"title":"Anthropic","url":"https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents"},{"title":"Writing a good CLAUDE.md | HumanLayer Blog","url":"https://www.humanlayer.dev/blog/writing-a-good-claude-md"},{"title":"Harness MCP Server (Beta) | Harness Developer Hub","url":"https://developer.harness.io/docs/platform/harness-aida/harness-mcp-server/"},{"title":"Building agents with the Claude Agent SDK","url":"https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/building-agents-with-the-claude-agent-sdk"},{"title":"How Claude Code is built - by Gergely Orosz","url":"https://newsletter.pragmaticengineer.com/p/how-claude-code-is-built"},{"title":"ccharness/CLAUDE.md at main · elct9620/ccharness","url":"https://github.com/elct9620/ccharness/blob/main/CLAUDE.md"},{"title":"✨ Feature Request: Support for Self-Hosted LLMs in Claude Code Harness · Issue #7178 · anthropics/claude-code","url":"https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/issues/7178"}]

Here are the search results for "Harness Protocol Claude Code":

The search reveals several relevant topics:

## 1. Harness MCP Server Integration
Harness now supports the Model Context Protocol (MCP) for Feature Management and Experimentation (FME), enabling developers to interact with feature flags directly from AI-powered IDEs like Claude Code and Windsurf.(no content)By integrating with Claude Code, Windsurf, Cursor, or VS Code, developers and product managers can discover projects, list feature flags, and inspect flag definitions, all without leaving their development environment.(no content)MCP (Model Context Protocol) is an open standard for AI agents to interact with tools and services using a unified protocol. Instead of building custom adapters or connectors for each tool, MCP provides a consistent interface that allows AI agents to communicate seamlessly with various tools, including Harness services. In simpler terms: MCP standardizes how AI agents talk to software tools, enabling interoperability without requiring custom code for each integration.

## 2. Claude Agent SDK (Harness as a Service)
The agent harness that powers Claude Code (the Claude Code SDK) can power many other types of agents, too. To reflect this broader vision, we're renaming the Claude Code SDK to the Claude Agent SDK.(no content)Built on top of the agent harness that powers Claude Code, the Claude Code SDK provides all the building blocks you need to build production-ready agents.

## 3. CCharness - A Lightweight Harness Tool
CCharness is a lightweight harness for Claude Code, built with TypeScript and designed to be a CLI tool that provides hooks and utilities for Claude Code operations.(no content)It intercepts and processes Claude Code hook events to provide additional functionality like commit reminders and decision handling.

## 4. Long-Running Agents Harness
We looked to human engineers for inspiration in creating a more effective harness for long-running agents. As AI agents become more capable, developers are increasingly asking them to take on complex tasks requiring work that spans hours, or even days. However, getting agents to make consistent progress across multiple context windows remains an open problem.

Would you like more specific information about any of these topics?

Web search results for query: "Harness Protocol AI development"

Links: [{"title":"Harness: AI for DevOps, Testing, AppSec, and Cost Optimization","url":"https://www.harness.io/"},{"title":"AI-Native Software Delivery for DevOps","url":"https://www.harness.io/products/harness-ai"},{"title":"Harness Adds MCP Server to Expose Data to Third-Party AI Tools - DevOps.com","url":"https://devops.com/harness-adds-mcp-server-to-expose-data-to-third-party-ai-tools/"},{"title":"Harness MCP Server (Beta) | Harness Developer Hub","url":"https://developer.harness.io/docs/platform/harness-aida/harness-mcp-server/"},{"title":"Overview of Harness AI | Harness Developer Hub","url":"https://developer.harness.io/docs/platform/harness-aida/aida-overview/"},{"title":"The Harness MCP Server | Blog","url":"https://www.harness.io/blog/mcp-announcement"},{"title":"AI/ML in the Harness Platform","url":"https://www.harness.io/resources/ai-ml-in-the-harness-platform"},{"title":"The Three Tools Shaping AI’s Future: Understanding Petri, MCP, and Harnesses | by Graham Wallington | Medium","url":"https://medium.com/@graham.wallington/the-three-tools-shaping-ais-future-understanding-petri-mcp-and-harnesses-37b27199f212"},{"title":"Harness AI Tackles Software Development's Real Bottleneck - The New Stack","url":"https://thenewstack.io/harness-ai-tackles-software-developments-real-bottleneck/"},{"title":"Anthropic","url":"https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents"}]

Based on my search, I found relevant information about Harness and protocols related to AI development. Here's what I found:

## Harness Platform - AI for DevOps

Harness AI is a core feature of the Harness Platform and offers a multi-agent approach and AI-powered features to improve all aspects of Software Delivery, from coding to building and deploying software.

### Model Context Protocol (MCP) Integration

Harness has developed a model context protocol (MCP) server through which it will be able to provide more context to artificial intelligence (AI) tools, agents and platforms that are being used to build software.(no content)Originally developed by Anthropic, MCP is based on a JSON-RPC 2.0 API framework and is rapidly becoming a de facto API standard for integrating AI tools, agents and platforms.(no content)MCP (Model Context Protocol) is an open standard for AI agents to interact with tools and services using a unified protocol. Instead of building custom adapters or connectors for each tool, MCP provides a consistent interface that allows AI agents to communicate seamlessly with various tools.

### Harness MCP Server Capabilities

By exposing a growing set of structured, secure tool sets—including pipelines, repositories, logs, and artifact registries—MCP gives agents consistent access to the same systems your teams already use to build, test, and deploy software.(no content)The MCP server supports seamless integration with third-party tools like Windsurf, Cursor, and Claude Desktop, eliminating the need for custom adapters.

### Harness AI Features

Harness AI offers comprehensive runtime awareness capabilities that go far beyond code completion—empowering developers and DevOps teams to manage, automate, and optimize their software delivery processes with precision.(no content)Instead of building another point solution, Harness created what it calls a "Software Delivery Knowledge Graph" — essentially a brain that understands your entire development process, from code repos to production infrastructure.

### Broader Context: AI Development Harnesses

Separately, as AI agents become more capable, developers are increasingly asking them to take on complex tasks requiring work that spans hours, or even days. However, getting agents to make consistent progress across multiple context windows remains an open problem.(no content)The Claude Agent SDK is a powerful, general-purpose agent harness adept at coding, as well as other tasks that require the model to use tools to gather context, plan, and execute. It has context management capabilities such as compaction, which enables an agent to work on a task without exhausting the context window.

Links: [{"title":"Get started with Claude Code hooks - Claude Code Docs","url":"https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks-guide"},{"title":"GitHub - elct9620/ccharness: A lightweight harness for Claude Code","url":"https://github.com/elct9620/ccharness"},{"title":"Hooks reference - Claude Docs","url":"https://docs.claude.com/en/docs/claude-code/hooks"},{"title":"GitHub - disler/claude-code-hooks-mastery","url":"https://github.com/disler/claude-code-hooks-mastery"},{"title":"ccharness/CLAUDE.md at main · elct9620/ccharness","url":"https://github.com/elct9620/ccharness/blob/main/CLAUDE.md"},{"title":"Claude Code Hooks: What is and How to Use It - CometAPI - All AI Models in One API","url":"https://www.cometapi.com/claude-code-hooks-what-is-and-how-to-use-it/"},{"title":"Automate Your AI Workflows with Claude Code Hooks | Butler's Log","url":"https://blog.gitbutler.com/automate-your-ai-workflows-with-claude-code-hooks"},{"title":"What is Claude Code Hooks and How to Use It","url":"https://apidog.com/blog/claude-code-hooks/"},{"title":"ClaudeLog - Claude Code Docs, Guides, Tutorials & Best Practices","url":"https://claudelog.com/mechanics/hooks/"},{"title":"GitHub - disler/claude-code-hooks-multi-agent-observability: Real-time monitoring for Claude Code agents through simple hook event tracking.","url":"https://github.com/disler/claude-code-hooks-multi-agent-observability"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Claude Code harness hooks:

## Claude Code Hooks Overview

Claude Code hooks are user-defined shell commands that execute at various points in Claude Code's lifecycle.(no content)Hooks provide deterministic control over Claude Code's behavior, ensuring certain actions always happen rather than relying on the LLM to choose to run them.

### Hook Events

Claude Code provides several hook events that run at different points in the workflow: PreToolUse (runs before tool calls and can block them), PermissionRequest (runs when a permission dialog is shown), and SessionEnd (runs when Claude Code session ends).

Additional hook events include:
- PreToolUse - Before tool execution, PostToolUse - After tool completion, UserPromptSubmit - When user submits a prompt.
- Stop - When Claude Code agent finishes responding, SubagentStop - When a subagent finishes (v1.0.41+), SessionEnd - When Claude Code session terminates.

### CCharness - A Lightweight Harness

There's a specific tool called **CCharness** that provides harness functionality for Claude Code:

CCharness is a lightweight harness for Claude Code, built with TypeScript and designed to be a CLI tool that provides hooks and utilities for Claude Code operations.(no content)It intercepts and processes Claude Code hook events to provide additional functionality like commit reminders and decision handling.(no content)Use npx -y @aotoki/ccharness <command> to run commands without installing globally.

#### CCharness Hook Commands

- A Stop hook to ensure Claude Code makes a commit when throttled: `npx -y @aotoki/ccharness hook guard-commit`
- A PostToolUse hook for Write, Edit, MultiEdit tool to remind Claude Code to review with rubric(s): `npx -y @aotoki/ccharness hook review-reminder`
- A PreToolUse hook for the Read tool to restrict Claude Code's access to sensitive files. This hook prevents Claude Code from reading files that match configured sensitive patterns. When a restricted file is accessed, the hook denies permission and explains the reason.

### Hook Communication

Hooks communicate status through exit codes, stdout, and stderr: Exit code 0 means Success, and stdout is shown to the user in transcript mode (CTRL-R), except for UserPromptSubmit and SessionStart, where stdout is added to the context.

### Security Considerations

You must consider the security implication of hooks as you add them, because hooks run automatically during the agent loop with your current environment's credentials. For example, malicious hooks code can exfiltrate your data.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

content.text:
Based on my search results, here's a comprehensive summary of the Harness Protocol and related concepts in Claude Code development:

## What is the "Harness Protocol"?

The term "Harness Protocol" isn't a single formal standard, but rather refers to the **agent harness architecture** that powers Claude Code and the broader Claude Agent SDK. It's the underlying infrastructure that manages how AI agents like Claude Code operate, handle context, and execute tools over extended workflows.

## Key Components:

### 1. **Agent Harness Architecture**
The "harness" is the framework that manages:
- Long-running agent operations across multiple context windows
- Context management through compaction to prevent context window exhaustion
- Deterministic control flow for complex, multi-step tasks
- Hook execution at various lifecycle points

### 2. **Claude Code Hooks System**
Claude Code provides hooks—user-defined shell commands that execute at specific lifecycle points:
- **PreToolUse** - Blocks or allows tool execution before it happens
- **PostToolUse** - Runs after a tool completes
- **PermissionRequest** - Runs when permission dialogs appear
- **UserPromptSubmit** - When users submit prompts
- **SessionEnd** - When the session terminates

### 3. **CCharness Tool**
A practical implementation of the harness concept, CCharness is a TypeScript CLI tool that provides:
- Hook guard commands (e.g., `guard-commit` to force commits)
- Review reminders for file modifications
- Sensitive file access restrictions
- Can be invoked via `npx -y @aotoki/ccharness <command>`

### 4. **Model Context Protocol (MCP) Integration**
Harness (the DevOps company) has developed an MCP server enabling:
- Integration with Claude Code and other AI tools
- Access to feature flags, pipelines, and repositories
- Unified communication standard for AI agents

## Best Practices for Setup:

1. **Security First** - Hooks run with your current environment credentials, so validate hook code carefully
2. **Use Deterministic Hooks** - Replace reliance on LLM decision-making with guardrail hooks
3. **Hook Communication** - Understand exit codes and stdout/stderr for proper hook feedback
4. **Context Management** - Leverage the harness's context compaction for long-running tasks
5. **Integrate MCP servers** - Use the Model Context Protocol for better tool integration

## Documentation References:

- [Get started with Claude Code hooks - Claude Code Docs](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks-guide)
- [Claude Code Hooks Reference](https://docs.claude.com/en/docs/claude-code/hooks)
- [Anthropic - Effective Harnesses for Long-Running Agents](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents)
- [CCharness GitHub Repository](https://github.com/elct9620/ccharness)
- [Harness MCP Server Documentation](https://developer.harness.io/docs/platform/harness-aida/harness-mcp-server/)
- [Claude Code Hooks Best Practices](https://blog.gitbutler.com/automate-your-ai-workflows-with-claude-code-hooks)

The "Harness Protocol" is essentially the framework that enables reliable, long-running AI agent workflows by providing deterministic control points through hooks and robust context management capabilities.
